short: Anivar A. Aravind is an engineering executive and systems thinker. Twenty years across global commerce, RBI-regulated FinTech, and multilingual public computing. Currently Global Head of Engineering at Foodhub.
medium: Anivar A. Aravind is an engineering executive and systems thinker. Twenty years shipping software at population scale across global commerce, RBI-regulated FinTech, and multilingual public computing — including Senior Vice President, Engineering at Kinara Capital, and currently Global Head of Engineering at Foodhub. Co-author of Indian Standard IS 16333 (Part 3) on Indic-language computing. Founder of the Indic Project and Moving Republic. Contributor to Swathanthra Malayalam Computing since 2003. Independent writing and research on AI governance, public-sector technology, and the human layer of software.
long:
Anivar A. Aravind is an engineering executive and systems thinker. Over twenty years he has shipped software at population scale across global commerce, RBI-regulated FinTech, and multilingual public computing, in senior engineering roles at Paisool and Kinara Capital — where he was Senior Vice President, Engineering — and currently as Global Head of Engineering at Foodhub. His work sits at the seam where engineering has to ship commercially and hold up under regulator, board, and public scrutiny at once.

He is a co-author of Indian Standard IS 16333 (Part 3) on Indic-language computing and was named a Changemaker by Deccan Herald. He has contributed to Swathanthra Malayalam Computing since 2003 and served as its Coordinator & Secretary from 2007 to 2014. Earlier civic-tech work includes Executive Director of the Indic Project (2014 – 2018) and Founder & Executive Director of Moving Republic (2006 – 2012).

He sits on the Advisory Board of SFLC.in, is an ICANN Fellow and contributor since 2016, and is an Observer Member at MLCommons. Independent writing and research, listed under /projects/, covers AI governance, small open models, and the human layer of software.
